picks.rationale: The New York Knicks are in dominant form, boasting an 8-2 record in their last 10 games, including an active 8-game winning streak with an impressive +19.4 point differential. They defeated the Cavaliers by 11 points in Game 1 (115-104), comfortably covering a similar spread. Playing at home in Madison Square Garden, the Knicks carry significant momentum after their thrilling comeback victory. The Cavaliers, conversely, are 5-5 in their last 10 games and have a poor 2-5 ATS record recently.

Playoff games typically feature a slower pace and heightened defensive intensity, which tends to result in lower scoring. The Knicks' defense has been exceptional, allowing only 101.2 points per game in their last 10 contests. While Game 1 went to overtime, the regulation score of 102-102 (204 total points) would have easily stayed under the 215.5 line. The Cavaliers have also averaged a modest 109.0 points per game in their last 10 outings, further supporting an under.

The New York Knicks are on an impressive 8-game winning streak and have demonstrated superior form with an 8-2 record in their last 10 games. They secured a crucial Game 1 victory at home against the Cavaliers and hold a 3-2 advantage in their last five head-to-head matchups. With both teams fully healthy, the Knicks' strong momentum and home-court advantage at Madison Square Garden provide a significant edge. The moneyline movement, with the Knicks' odds shortening, also indicates sharp money favoring New York to win outright.

The Carolina Hurricanes enter this game on an impressive 8-0 playoff run, having swept their first two series and benefiting from a significant rest advantage over the Montreal Canadiens. Carolina boasts elite goaltending from Frederik Andersen, who leads the postseason with a 1.12 GAA and .950 SV%, complementing their formidable 95% penalty kill. While Montreal did win all three regular-season matchups, the playoff environment and Carolina's dominant defensive structure, which has attracted sharp money on their puck-line, are strong indicators for a Hurricanes victory in Game 1.

Carolina's defensive prowess is a major factor, as they've allowed just 1.3 goals per game in the playoffs and feature Frederik Andersen, who leads the league in playoff save percentage. Montreal, despite their offensive capabilities, averages the second-lowest shots per game in the postseason (25.1) and is coming off two physically demanding series, which could impact their offensive output. Sharp money has also moved towards alternate unders, recognizing that playoff hockey often features tighter, lower-scoring games, especially with Carolina's ability to suppress transition chances.

Despite Carolina's dominant playoff record, the Canadiens swept the regular season series against the Hurricanes by a combined score of 15-8, demonstrating their ability to compete effectively against this opponent. Montreal has proven their resilience by winning two consecutive seven-game series, showcasing their battle-tested nature and ability to perform under pressure. Furthermore, while the majority of public bets favor Carolina, 70% of the moneyline bets are actually on the Canadiens, indicating sharp money believes Montreal can keep this game close, even if they ultimately lose.

Taylor Hall has been a key offensive contributor for the Hurricanes in the playoffs, accumulating 12 points in 8 games, showcasing his impact in a middle-six role. He also had a strong regular season with 48 points in 80 games, and is confirmed in the projected lineup for today's game. Given Carolina's strong offensive system and their significant rest advantage, Hall is in a favorable position to register at least one point in Game 1. This prop is identified as a 'Best Prop' by sharp betting analysis, further increasing confidence in his ability to contribute offensively.

The Carolina Hurricanes have been dominant in the playoffs, boasting an 8-0 record with two series sweeps, indicating their ability to win convincingly. They also hold a significant rest advantage over the Canadiens, who are coming off two grueling seven-game series, which could lead to fatigue for Montreal. Carolina's strong defensive play and elite goaltending, allowing only 1.3 goals per game in the playoffs, combined with their average goal differential of +1.7, suggest they are well-positioned to cover the -1.5 spread at home. Sharp money has also shown a lean towards Carolina's puck-line, further supporting this pick.

picks.game: New York Mets vs Washington Nationals
picks.rationale: The Washington Nationals offense has been the best in MLB, averaging 5.58 runs per game, and has scored at least seven runs in four of their last five games. The Mets offense has also shown recent signs of life, averaging 7.0 runs per game over their last nine outings. Both teams have seen the Over hit in recent games, with the Nationals' last six games as underdogs going Over, and the Mets' last five games going Over. With Mets starter David Peterson having struggled against the Nationals previously this season, allowing seven runs in 3 2/3 innings, and the low total of 5.5, the Over presents significant value.

The Atlanta Braves, with the best record in MLB at 34-16, have demonstrated their superior form by winning two consecutive games against the Marlins after an initial shutout loss, outscoring them 17-5. Spencer Strider, the Braves' starting pitcher, has been in excellent form, allowing just one earned run over his last 11.1 innings pitched across two starts. While Marlins' ace Sandy Alcántara has a better home ERA, the Braves' offense is elite, leading MLB with a .441 slugging percentage. The return of Ronald Acuna Jr. further strengthens an already potent Braves lineup.

picks.game: Toronto Blue Jays vs New York Yankees
picks.rationale: The New York Yankees boast a strong home record of 16-7 this season, providing a significant advantage in this series finale. While Yankees starter Carlos Rodon has a 5.63 ERA, the Toronto Blue Jays offense has been struggling significantly, ranking as the fourth-worst in wRC+ over the past 30 days and 20th against left-handed pitching. The Blue Jays are also 9-16 on the road this season, further highlighting their struggles away from home. Despite some recent offensive struggles, the Yankees have a deeper lineup capable of capitalizing on the Blue Jays' bullpen game approach after their opener.

The Athletics are in better form with a 25-24 record and have won the last two games of this series against the struggling Angels. Athletics' starting pitcher Luis Severino has shown significantly better performance on the road this season, boasting a a 3.56 ERA compared to his 5.55 ERA at home. Conversely, Angels starter Jose Soriano is coming off a tough outing where he allowed six runs in 5.1 innings, suggesting potential for regression. The Angels have a dismal 17-33 record and have lost 7 of their last 10 games, making the Athletics moneyline at -102 a favorable pick against a team in disarray.

The Arizona Diamondbacks are in excellent form, riding a four-game winning streak and holding a strong 15-9 home record this season. They face a struggling Colorado Rockies team that has lost two straight and holds a 19-31 overall record. The pitching matchup heavily favors Arizona, with Eduardo Rodriguez (4-1, 2.53 ERA) on the mound against Zach Agnos (0-0, 5.59 ERA) for the Rockies. Furthermore, the Rockies have one of the worst offenses against left-handed pitchers this season, which bodes well for Rodriguez. The Diamondbacks have also dominated this head-to-head matchup at home, winning nine of their last ten games against the Rockies in Arizona.
